LABEL.Definition: the name used to describe him

WANDERS.Definition: walks around without a clear plan

AGITATED.Definition: upset, nervous, and unable to stay calm

INCOHERENT.Definition: hard to understand; not making sense

COURTIERS.Definition: people who worked or lived around him

COLONISTS.Definition: people living in countries controlled by a more powerful country

COME CRAWLING BACK.Definition: return in a weak or sorry way

RAVING.Definition: talking wildly and not making sense

TURBULENT.Definition: full of trouble, change, or fighting

EXTRAVAGANT.Definition: spending too much money or using too much

OBSESSED WITH.Definition: thinking about them all the time

OBSERVATORY.Definition: a place for looking at stars and planets

PEN NAME.Definition: a fake name used by a writer

IMPLICATION.Definition: a meaning that was suggested but not said directly

EARTHY.Definition: simple and close to ordinary life or farming

ARRANGED MARRIAGE.Definition: a marriage planned by other people

DUTIFUL.Definition: doing what he was expected to do

THRONE.Definition: the special seat of a king or queen; royal power

CUNNING.Definition: being clever in a tricky or sneaky way

INTERNAL UNREST.Definition: trouble, anger, or fighting inside the country

LANDED WITH.Definition: given something difficult to deal with

DOUBLE-EDGED SWORD.Definition: something with both good and bad results

DROWNING IN DEBT.Definition: owing a very large amount of money

INTENTIONS.Definition: plans or reasons for doing something

CONSTITUTIONAL MONARCH.Definition: a king whose power was limited by law

STUBBORN.Definition: not willing to change his mind

SPECTATOR.Definition: someone who watched but did not take part

TURNIPS.Definition: round white or purple root vegetables

ASTRONOMY.Definition: the study of stars and planets

PRESIDING OVER.Definition: being in charge while it happened

TUMBLING OVER EACH OTHER.Definition: coming out very quickly and messily

DARK TURN.Definition: a change to something sad, serious, or frightening

INTERVENTIONS.Definition: actions taken to help the situation

BLEEDING.Definition: taking blood from him as a treatment

LAXATIVES.Definition: medicines that made it easier to empty the bowels

EMETICS.Definition: medicines that made him vomit or throw up

BLISTERING AGENTS.Definition: chemicals that made painful bubbles on the skin

CAUSTIC.Definition: able to burn or damage skin

STRAITJACKET.Definition: a jacket that tied his arms so he could not move

GAGGED.Definition: stopped from speaking by covering the mouth

IRONY.Definition: a situation that was strange because it was the opposite of what is expected

PRACTITIONER.Definition: someone who did a skilled job, like a doctor

INSTITUTIONS.Definition: large places or organisations for care or treatment

ROT.Definition: slowly decay; be left in a terrible state

ROYAL ASSENT.Definition: the king or queen’s official agreement to a new law

MOOT.Definition: no longer important or useful to discuss

WANDERED.Definition: walked around without a clear plan

GAUNT.Definition: very thin because of illness or age

METABOLIC.Definition: connected to how his body used food and energy

PSYCHIATRIC.Definition: connected to mental illness or the mind

UNDERLYING CONDITION.Definition: health problem that was already there

SYSTEMATIC REVIEW.Definition: a careful study of many facts or records

SELECTIVE.Definition: choosing only some things and ignoring others

DOWNPLAYED.Definition: made them seem less important than they were

BIPOLAR DISORDER.Definition: a mental illness with very high and very low moods

MANIC.Definition: having very high energy and thoughts that are too fast

DELUSIONS.Definition: false beliefs that felt real to him

MANIA.Definition: a state of very high energy, fast thoughts, and unusual behaviour

EXAGGERATED.Definition: made to seem bigger or more serious than it was

PURGING.Definition: forcing the body to empty itself, often by vomiting or using the toilet

BLISTERING.Definition: making painful bubbles on the skin

RESTRAINED.Definition: held or tied so he could not move freely

CARICATURES.Definition: simple, unfair, or funny pictures of people that show only one side

CONSCIENTIOUS.Definition: careful and wanting to do the right thing
